asset.title: Noderoom Live Collab V1
asset.purpose: Narrated explainer episode. Judge story clarity, visual design, proof quality, and whether it fits the README claim.

judge.summary: An excellent, highly polished narrated explainer demonstrating multiplayer AI collaboration on a spreadsheet. It clearly explains the technical challenges of state synchronization and locking with code and diagrams.

featureClarity.evidence: The video clearly demonstrates a shared spreadsheet workspace where both a human and an AI agent collaborate and edit cells.

workflowCompleteness.evidence: Shows the step-by-step process of editing, conflict detection, code-level resolution, and final approval.

visualDesign.evidence: Features a clean dark-themed UI, well-structured layout, and clear conceptual diagrams explaining the lock/draft mechanism.

consistency.evidence: Perfectly aligns with the NodeRoom narrative of multi-agent collaboration, shared artifacts, and execution traces.

evidenceQuality.evidence: Provides authentic product footage combined with actual backend code snippets and architecture flows.

legibility.evidence: Most UI elements and captions are highly readable, though the code snippet at 00:18 is slightly dense.

professionalRelevance.evidence: Highly valuable for software engineers and AI product builders dealing with multi-agent state synchronization.

productionHonesty.evidence: Transparently shows the limitations of naive agent writes and explains the necessity of a proposal/review system.

observedEvidence: Multiplayer spreadsheet UI with real-time cell updates
Chat interface showing agent and human messages
Code snippet showing cell edit locking logic
Conceptual diagram of the collaboration substrate

defects.observed: The code block showing the locking logic is dense and the font size is small, making it slightly hard to read quickly.
defects.fix: Apply a subtle zoom-in effect on the active lines of code being discussed.

judge.suggestedReadmeCaption: Watch how Noderoom handles real-time multiplayer collaboration between humans and AI agents, resolving state conflicts using a robust lock-and-proposal substrate.
